notes: Header: kill the empty gold box left of the SYS pill under redesign. Root cause (diagnosed live in Chrome on a clean desktop boot, served .163/.164): the legacy header overflow/ghost button #hdr-ghost-btn sits inside #hdr-overflow-wrap in its .alert state (gold rgba(255,214,10,0.35) border+glow, set by identity_refreshOverflowLabels when no identity is set); its only content .hdr-ghost-img renders 0x0 in that view, so the button shows as an empty gold-bordered box. The .156 medallion-retirement added `body.rd #hdr-overflow-wrap{display:none}` to hide exactly this under redesign, but the element's inline style="display:inline-block" (+ a JS display:block) silently defeated the non-!important rule, so it never actually hid -- the box has leaked since .156. FIX: one word -- `display:none` -> `display:none !important` on the existing body.rd rule, which beats the inline/JS display and lands the intended hide. Redesign identity cue still lives in the gold Profile status pill. NOT net-pill/SYS (fine, cyan, state=ok), NOT #phantom-tab-pill/#sw-status-pill (correctly display:none), NOT stale cache/glare. body.rd-scoped, CSS-only (one word + comment), no JS touched. ?legacy=1 byte-identical (legacy still shows the ghost/overflow button + its alert). DEVICE-VERIFY (HARD-STOP): redesign header -- no empty box left of SYS; SYS pill unchanged; the 'set identity/profile' state still surfaces via the Profile pill; ?legacy=1 ghost button + overflow menu still work. ROLLBACK: revert the commit.
